f4ce7368-bd93-4c93-8e4b-96744da899ed.text:
Employee Policy Handbook
Version 1.0 | Effective Date: 1 January 2026 | Internal use for employee-app training
1. Employment Standards
Employees are expected to act with integrity, comply with applicable laws, protect company 
information, and treat colleagues, customers, and partners with respect. Managers are responsible 
for setting clear expectations, reviewing performance fairly, and escalating concerns through the 
appropriate channels.
Employment records must be accurate and current. Employees should promptly update changes 
to name, address, phone number, emergency contact, tax information, bank details, department, 
role, or reporting manager.
2. Working Hours and Attendance
The standard work week is 40 hours unless a different schedule is approved in writing. Core 
collaboration hours are 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM local office time, Monday through Friday. Flexible 
start and end times may be approved by the reporting manager when business coverage is 
maintained.

00d615ef-a0a3-4283-9ee0-9519d102fc7e.text:
collaboration hours are 10:00 AM to 4:00 PM local office time, Monday through Friday. Flexible 
start and end times may be approved by the reporting manager when business coverage is 
maintained.
• Employees should record attendance and leave accurately in the approved system. 
• Unplanned absence must be reported to the manager as early as reasonably possible. 
• Repeated late arrivals, unexplained absences, or inaccurate timesheets may result in 
corrective action. 
3. Leave Policy
Annual Leave
Full-time employees receive 18 working days of annual leave per calendar year. Leave should be 
requested at least 7 calendar days in advance, except in urgent circumstances. Approval depends 
on team coverage, project needs, and leave balance.
Sick Leave
Employees receive 10 working days of paid sick leave per calendar year. A medical certificate may 
be requested for absences longer than 2 consecutive working days or where required by local law.
Public Holidays

744757d2-9eb8-49b9-8e08-6af166c26c50.text:
be requested for absences longer than 2 consecutive working days or where required by local law.
Public Holidays
Employees are eligible for company-declared public holidays based on their assigned office 
location. Employees working on an approved holiday may receive compensatory time off or 
additional pay as determined by local policy.
Parental and Caregiver Leave
Parental, adoption, bereavement, and caregiver leave are provided according to local legal 
requirements and any enhanced company benefit published by Human Resources.
4. Remote and Hybrid Work
Remote or hybrid work may be approved when the employee's role, performance, data-security 
obligations, and team needs support it. Employees must maintain a suitable working environment, 
reliable internet access, and availability during agreed working hours.
• Company devices must be secured when unattended. 
• Confidential discussions should not take place in public or shared spaces.

36dda286-2313-44c2-8398-8b89777ab844.text:
• Company devices must be secured when unattended. 
• Confidential discussions should not take place in public or shared spaces. 
• Employees must use approved collaboration, authentication, and file-sharing tools. 
5. Compensation and Payroll
Salary is paid monthly to the bank account recorded in the employee profile. Payroll changes, 
reimbursements, overtime payments, and deductions must be supported by approved 
documentation. Employees should report payroll discrepancies to Human Resources or Finance 
within 5 business days of payment.
6. Performance and Development
Performance is reviewed through regular manager feedback, goal setting, and periodic formal 
reviews. Goals should be specific, measurable, relevant to the role, and aligned with department 
objectives. Employees are encouraged to discuss learning needs, career interests, and blockers 
with their managers early.
Promotions and role changes are based on sustained performance, scope of responsibility,

87aa1d40-3c69-48e7-9717-a7a43535b75f.text:
with their managers early.
Promotions and role changes are based on sustained performance, scope of responsibility, 
business need, conduct, and demonstrated readiness for the next level.
7. Code of Conduct
Employees must maintain a workplace free from harassment, discrimination, retaliation, bullying, 
intimidation, and unsafe behavior. Concerns may be reported to a manager, Human Resources, or 
the designated ethics contact. Reports will be reviewed as confidentially as practical, and 
retaliation against any person who raises a concern in good faith is prohibited.
• Use respectful language in meetings, chat, email, and code reviews. 
• Declare conflicts of interest involving vendors, candidates, customers, or outside work. 
• Do not accept gifts, favors, or entertainment that could influence business decisions. 
8. Information Security
Employees are responsible for protecting company data, customer data, credentials, source code,

74ec1f0b-f57a-40a8-becf-44cf927291cb.text:
8. Information Security
Employees are responsible for protecting company data, customer data, credentials, source code, 
financial records, and personal information. Access is granted according to job responsibilities and 
may be reviewed or revoked when duties change.
• Use strong passwords and multi-factor authentication where required. 
• Do not share accounts, tokens, API keys, or passwords. 
• Report suspected phishing, malware, lost devices, or unauthorized access immediately. 
• Store documents only in approved company systems. 
9. Equipment and Company Property
Company property, including laptops, access cards, phones, software licenses, and documents, 
must be used responsibly and returned when employment ends or when requested. Employees 
should not install unauthorized software, bypass security controls, or use company assets for 
unlawful activity.
10. Expenses and Travel
Business expenses must be reasonable, necessary, and pre-approved where required. Expense

f090fbb1-0704-43b3-8efb-50ad705fc3c5.text:
unlawful activity.
10. Expenses and Travel
Business expenses must be reasonable, necessary, and pre-approved where required. Expense 
claims should include receipts and be submitted within 30 calendar days. Travel should be 
planned with attention to safety, cost, business need, and local approval requirements.
11. Health and Safety
Employees must follow workplace health and safety instructions, report hazards, and cooperate 
with emergency procedures. Injuries, unsafe conditions, or security incidents should be reported 
as soon as possible to the manager or facilities contact.
12. Employee Data and Privacy
The company collects and processes employee information for employment administration, 
payroll, benefits, security, compliance, and business operations. Employee data should be 
accessed only by authorized personnel and handled according to privacy and retention 
requirements.
13. Disciplinary Action

80050dc4-c967-4c2a-b127-04494a08726c.text:
accessed only by authorized personnel and handled according to privacy and retention 
requirements.
13. Disciplinary Action
Policy violations may result in coaching, written warning, suspension, loss of access, termination 
of employment, or legal action depending on severity, intent, impact, and prior conduct. Serious 
misconduct may lead to immediate termination where permitted by law.
14. Separation and Offboarding
Employees leaving the company must complete offboarding tasks, return company property, hand 
over work materials, and maintain confidentiality after employment ends. Final pay, unused leave, 
benefits, and access removal will be handled according to local law and company procedure.
15. Policy Questions
Employees should contact Human Resources for questions about leave balances, benefits, payroll 
records, policy interpretation, or workplace concerns. Managers should consult Human Resources 
before making exceptions to policy or taking corrective action.
